english: sure, dben-ynás (dben gnas) Mil. a safe, inaccessible retreat; rdzoṅ btsan (rdzong btsan) a firm stronghold Lex.; = concealed, hidden, hence btsan-kaṅ (btsan khang) the innermost dark room in a temple, in which the gods reside, or an apartment for the same purpose on the top of a house; definite, decided, without uncertainty, saṅs-rgyás-kyi bstán-pa mi núb-ciṅ mta btsán-par byéd-pai pyir (sangs rgyas kyi bstan pa mi nub cing mtha' btsan par byed pa'i phyir) in order that the doctrine of Buddha by being accurately defined may be secured against subversion Pth.
imp. btsob (btsob), to cut small, to chop, wood; to hash, to mince, meat C.; bstab-stán (bstab stan) chopping-block C.

1. also rtsás-ma (rtsas ma) harvest, btsás-ma rṅa-ba (btsas ma rnga ba) to reap, to mow C. and Lex., btsás-ma ran tsa-na (btsas ma ran tsa na) in harvest time Mil. — 2. wages, pay, gru-btsás (gru btsas) Lex., fare, passage-money; la-btsás (la btsas) Lex., la-ċan-gyi btsas (la can gyi btsas)?

1. respectable, noble, of race, family, rigs ċé-ziṅ btsún-pa (rigs che zhing btsun pa) id. Dzl.; btsún-pai bud-méd (btsun pa'i bud med) Dzl. a lady of rank. — 2. reverend, as title of ecclesiastics, btsún-pa-rnams (btsun pa rnams) the ecclesiastics, priests Glr., = ban-dhe (ban dhe) and Ssk. भदन्त (Tar. Transl. p. 4, note 7); even btsún-pa krims-méd (btsun pa khrims med) wicked Reverends Ma. — 3. creditable, honourable, faithful in observing religious duties, so frq.: mkas btsun bzaṅ ysum (mkhas btsun bzang gsum) v. mkas-pa (mkhas pa); tsig-btsún-pa (tshig btsun pa) grave and virtuous discourse Schr., Sch.: polite words(?), tsig mi btsun-pa (tshig mi btsun pa) Thgy. was explained to me: one whom nobody believes; applied to things: good; thus Mil. says of his cane: spa ċo-ris yé-nas btsún-pa de (spa cho ris ye nas btsun pa de) this cane of quite an excellent quality. — btsún-po (btsun po) = btsún-pa (btsun pa) 1., rgya-rjé btsún-po (rgya rje btsun po) the noble emperor of China Glr.; as a title v. snyuṅ (snyung); btsún-por byéd-pa (btsun por byed pa) Cs. to reverence. — btsún-ma (btsun ma) priestess Cs. — btsún-mo (btsun mo) 1. woman of rank, a lady; also as a term of address: your ladyship, e.g. in a legend, when a merchant speaks to the wife of a judge Dzl.; spouse, consort, esp. queen consort, with and without rgyál-poi (rgyal po'i), frq.; btsún-mo ċé-ba (btsun mo che ba) = ċen-ma (chen ma) the principal wife; btsun-mo-ċan (btsun mo can) having a wife, btsun-mo-méd (btsun mo med) not having a wife Cs. — 2. nun, mo-btsún (mo btsun), id. Glr., C.

purification, refining(?) *ser-la tso taṅ-wa* (*gser la btso btang ba*) C. to refine gold (which term eventually is the same as 'to boil') v. °tsod-pa ('tsod pa); btsó-ma (btso ma), btsós-ma (btsos ma) a purified substance, yser btsó-ma (gser btso ma), purified gold, very frq. with regard to a bright yellow colour Glr.
v. °tsód-pa ('tsod pa); btso-blág-pa (btso blag pa) to dye, to colour, btso-blág-mkan (btso blag mkhan) a dyer, Lex.

L vb. v. °tsog-pa ('tsog pa). II. adj., also (b)rtsóg(s)-pa ((b)rtsog(s) pa), W. *sóg-po* (*sog po*) 1. unclean, dirty, nasty, vile, °dí-ni śiṅ-tu rtsóg-pai sa yin ('di ni shin tu rtsog pa'i sa yin) this is a very vile place, says the prince of hades to a saint visiting there; so also every Tibetan will say to a stranger entering his house; ṅa btsog-ċiṅ (nga btsog cing) when I am getting unclean, i.e. when I am confined Dzl.; lus btsog-pa mnyam-pa °di (lus btsog pa mnyam pa 'di) this vile stinking body Dzl. — 2. in W. the common word for bad in every respect, useless, spoiled, troublesome, perilous (e.g. of a road); injurious; also in a more relative sense, inferior, poor, of goods; btsog-nág (btsog nag) tobacco-juice, oil from the tobacco-pipe.
onion Med. and vulgo, eschewed by pious Buddhists and ascetics, but a favourite food of the bulk of the people: btsoṅ srég-pa (btsong sreg pa) to roast onions.
